reasoning: Liu Zige's 200m butterfly long course world record (2:01.81) was set in 2009 during the super-suit era and has not been closely approached by anyone except Summer McIntosh. Although McIntosh swam a personal best of 2:03.03 at the Paris 2024 Olympics, she remains 1.22 seconds away from Liu's record. This is an enormous margin in a 200m event. Given the difficulty of the shiny-suit era records, it is highly likely that this record will stand until at least November 2025. Although McIntosh has the 2025 World Championships in Singapore as an opportunity, a drop of over 1.2 seconds in roughly a year is highly improbable.
